findings.title: 500-level errors silently counted as skipped combos
findings.why_it_matters: run_backtest() wraps every non-HTTP exception in a 500 HTTPException (backtest.py lines 934-937). The optimizer inner except catches all HTTPException including 500s, increments skipped, and continues. A bad ticker, yfinance outage, or any data-layer error causes every combo to be silently dropped; the endpoint returns HTTP 200 with an empty results list and skipped=200. The caller cannot distinguish no-valid-trades from data-source-down.
evidence: backtest_optimizer.py lines 283-296: except HTTPException: skipped += 1 catches all HTTPException statuses indiscriminately
backtest.py lines 934-937: except Exception as e: raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e)) — run_backtest converts every error including network/data failures into a 500 HTTPException
A yfinance fetch error on every combo produces OptimizeResponse(results=[], total_combos=200, completed=0, skipped=200) with HTTP 200
findings.suggested_fix: Only swallow 4xx HTTPExceptions as skipped; re-raise 5xx. In the inner except: if e.status_code >= 500: raise. Data-layer failures then surface immediately rather than being silently absorbed.

findings.title: Redundant deep model_copy per combo iteration
findings.why_it_matters: The optimizer calls req.base.model_copy(deep=True) before each _apply_param, but _apply_param itself immediately deep-copies its input as its first statement. The outer copy is discarded on the first call. With up to 200 combos and multiple params this is up to 400 wasted deep copies of a potentially large StrategyRequest.
evidence: backtest_optimizer.py line 277: modified = req.base.model_copy(deep=True) — creates a deep copy immediately before the loop
backtest_sweep.py line 53: modified = base.model_copy(deep=True) — _apply_param deep-copies its input as its first statement, making the callers prior copy dead work
backtest_optimizer.py lines 278-279: modified = _apply_param(modified, path, value) — first call discards the outer copy
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the outer model_copy and pass req.base directly to the first _apply_param: replace the outer copy line with modified = req.base, then loop as-is. _apply_param handles the copy internally.

findings.title: Non-deterministic set formatting in validation error message
findings.why_it_matters: f-string interpolation of a set produces ordering that varies between Python runs, making the validation error message non-deterministic across server restarts. Minor but visible quality issue for clients that display or parse the detail string.
evidence: backtest_optimizer.py line 252: f"metric must be one of: {_VALID_METRICS}" — set literals have no guaranteed iteration order
findings.suggested_fix: Use sorted(_VALID_METRICS) in the f-string: f"metric must be one of: {sorted(_VALID_METRICS)}"

findings.title: run_backtest missing return type annotation
findings.why_it_matters: run_backtest() is consumed by the new optimizer via implicit dict access. Without a TypedDict or return annotation, the optimizer result["summary"] access and .get() calls are untyped. A future shape change is not statically catchable.
evidence: backtest.py line 296: def run_backtest(req: StrategyRequest): — no return type
backtest_optimizer.py lines 285-293: s = result["summary"] then multiple s.get(...) calls — consuming an untyped dict shape

residual_risks: The 200-combo synchronous loop runs on the main FastAPI event loop and blocks all other requests. At 200ms per backtest, a single optimize call ties the server up for ~40 seconds. No async pathway or timeout guard exists in this module.

The completed field in OptimizeResponse is len(results) before the top_n slice. This is correct but non-obvious — a future maintainer moving the assignment after the slice would break the semantics silently.
testing_gaps: No test for 500-vs-4xx discrimination: a ticker causing a data-fetch error should make the endpoint return 500, not HTTP 200 with skipped=N.
No test verifying all 200 combos are enumerated at the exact boundary (10x10x2 = 200 allowed, 10x10x3 = 300 rejected).
No test for the fail-fast path where a valid-values param is paired with an unsupported param_path across combos.
No test asserting sort order correctness for each of the three supported metrics.
